extensions.title: Syntax highlighting
extensions.description: Automatically detects the language of your code and applies clear, readable syntax highlighting. The plugin analyzes the content and highlights it without requiring manual configuration. It improves code readability by coloring tags, keywords, strings, comments, and attributes inline, making embedded code snippets easier to scan and understand.
extensions.author: Front Labs

markup.description: Applies syntax highlighting to the element's content. Automatically detects HTML, JavaScript, or shell language.
Formats and indents the element's content, then applies syntax highlighting.

config.description: Comma-separated colors for HTML highlighting: brackets, tags, attributes, values, comments.
Comma-separated colors for JavaScript highlighting: keywords, strings, comments, functions, other.
Comma-separated colors for shell script highlighting.
Background color used to highlight syntax errors in editable mode.
Indentation string used when formatting code (default: two spaces).

extensions.title: Clipboard
extensions.description: Easily copy, paste, and manage text directly from the application. The Clipboard plugin allows users to quickly copy content to the system clipboard and paste it with a single action, making it simple to reuse text across documents, forms, and external tools. It improves productivity by removing manual selection and streamlining common copy and paste workflows.
extensions.author: Front Labs

markup.description: Copies the text content of the element to the system clipboard. Accepts a selector to copy from a different element.
Pastes text from the system clipboard into the element. Accepts a selector to paste into a different element.
When set to 'true', copies only the currently selected text range instead of the full element content.

config.description: Global default for range mode. When 'true', clipboard--copy will use the active text selection if available.

extensions.title: Scrollbar
extensions.description: Adds a customizable scrollbar to your application, allowing you to control appearance, size, and behavior. The Scrollbar plugin improves navigation in scrollable content areas by providing smooth scrolling and consistent styling across different browsers and devices.
extensions.author: Front Labs

markup.description: Applies custom scrollbar styles to the element. Accepts inline config as semicolon-separated key:value pairs (e.g. thumb:red;track:black;width:thin).

config.description: Scrollbar width. Values: 'thin', 'auto', 'none', or a pixel number.
Border radius in pixels for the scrollbar thumb.
Color of the scrollbar thumb.
Color of the scrollbar track.
Color of the scrollbar thumb on hover.

extensions.title: ResponsiveVoice
extensions.description: Adds text-to-speech functionality using ResponsiveVoice service by responsivevoice.org, allowing your app to read text aloud in multiple languages and voices.
extensions.author: Josef Gabrielsson

markup.description: Speaks the given text aloud using the ResponsiveVoice text-to-speech engine. Value is the text to speak.

extensions.title: Filter Search
extensions.description: The Filter Search plugin lets users instantly narrow down lists or sections by typing a search term. It dynamically shows or hides matching elements and automatically updates counters per section, giving immediate visual feedback. This makes it ideal for large lists, menus, dictionaries, or categorized content where quick filtering and clarity are important.
extensions.author: Josef Gabrielsson

markup.description: Marks an element as a filterable row. No value required.
Groups the row under a named section counter. Value is the counter key, e.g. filtersearch--count="count1".
Placed on the container. Shows the target element when no rows match, hides it otherwise. Value is a selector, e.g. filtersearch--onempty="#emptyMessage".
Triggers the filter search. Typically used with onvaluechange on an input. Value is an optional container selector to scope the search.
Marks an input as the filter source when using a scoped container. No value required.
Placed on a checkbox input. Groups filter rows by category. Checked checkboxes activate filtering by the group value.
Marks a child element inside a row as the value indicator for group-based filtering.

extensions.title: Particles
extensions.description: Bring your page to life with a dynamic particles. This plugin adds configurable and colorful particles that drift downward and subtly follow your mouse movements, creating a lively and interactive effect. You can enjoy a customizable, animated backdrop that makes your content feel more engaging and visually appealing.
extensions.author: Josef Gabrielsson

markup.description: Loads and starts the particle animation inside the element.

config.description: Total number of particles to render (max 500).
Maximum vertical speed of particles.
Size in pixels of each particle (max 50).
Comma-separated list of colors for particles.
Enable or disable mouse tracking. Particles drift toward cursor when true.

extensions.title: Map
extensions.description: Integrate Google Maps into your application, allowing you to display interactive maps, add markers, routes, and locations. This plugin makes it easy to enhance your app with geolocation features and visual map data.
extensions.author: Josef Gabrielsson

markup.description: Sets the latitude coordinate for the map center and marker.
Sets the longitude coordinate for the map center and marker.
Initializes and renders the map on the target element.

config.description: Google Maps API key for authentication.
Default target element selector for Google Maps rendering.

extensions.title: Reading Time
extensions.description: Calculates and displays the estimated reading time of a target article or text block based on word count and customizable Words Per Minute (WPM) settings.
extensions.author: Josef Gabrielsson

markup.description: The CSS selector (e.g., '.article-body' or '#content') of the element containing the text to be analyzed for reading time.

config.description: Words Per Minute. The average reading speed used to calculate the estimated time.
The text displayed when the estimated reading time is under one minute.
The text appended when the estimated reading time is exactly one minute (e.g., '1 min read').
The text appended when the estimated reading time is two or more minutes (e.g., '5 min to read').

extensions.title: Wave
extensions.description: Applies a dynamic, sliced horizontal wave animation effect to an image drawn over a responsive canvas wrapper.
extensions.author: Josef Gabrielsson

markup.description: The URL path of the source image to draw and animate on the canvas.
The total number of horizontal slice layers to split the image into for the wave calculation (Default: 40).
The incremental step rate added to the animation time delta on each frame loop (Default: 0.01).
The horizontal pixel intensity/amplitude multiplier of the Math.sin distortion effect (Default: 6).

config.description: Fallback global image URL path if wave-src isn't set explicitly on the targeted HTML element container.
Fallback global slice segments count for slicing the target graphic layout horizontally.
Fallback global rate variable used to speed up or slow down the sin wave calculation cycle.
Fallback global distance modifier controlling how far left and right the image slices distort.
